title: Why do you always come at night?
content:
Why do you always come at night?
In the deepest darker depths of night, silent
And hiding, under cunning disguise: you come, you go. Your
shame soaks my sheets so deep, your flame burns quiet,
But Oh, how it burns. Internal. External. Eternal.
I need you to come to me without jealousy. I need you to come to me without her eyes. Her eyes.
Her eyes.
They follow you. I feel them falling heavy through every window frame, bursting busting behind every door. I feel them against my back when I ride the thunder that is your thighs.
You hide from her in me.
You hide. You hide.
Come to me like you come to her.
In vigor and in power. In power and in vigor.
In definition. In aim. In purpose.
Come to me like you come to her.
Come to her like you once upon a time would come to me.
Never me, anymore.
Never me. Nor ever was.
She never sat where I could sit.
Never did. Never could. Next to him.
Never burdened nor buried by any of his disasters.
Never was. Never will be. Not for him.
